description_long: This imposing stone castle features four large cylindrical corner towers, each topped with crenellated battlements. The outer walls are robust and textured, enclosing a central courtyard with several inner buildings, some of which have steep blue-tiled conical roofs. The main entrance is marked by a prominent wooden gate set into the front wall, flanked by two of the towers. The castle's walls and towers are constructed from rough-hewn gray stone, giving it a fortified and defensive appearance. The blue roofs add a touch of color and contrast to the otherwise dark and stony structure. The overall design is reminiscent of medieval European castles, built for both defense and grandeur.
description: A fortified stone castle with four round corner towers, blue-tiled conical roofs, and a central courtyard, exuding a classic medieval appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance gate, two prominent corner towers, and blue-roofed inner buildings.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back wall, all four towers, and the blue-tiled roofs of the inner structures.
description_view_2: Side view with the entrance gate visible, two corner towers, and a clear look at the blue-roofed buildings inside.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the back wall, all four towers, and the arrangement of the inner blue-roofed buildings.
